title: Named Entity Recognition Pipelines
concept: NER tracks entities (Person, Org, Location) across sentences; the model outputs a label per token using a softmax layer at each time step.

exercises.prompt: In the context of language processing, what does the term "Named Entity" refer to?
exercises.explain: Entities in NLP refer to real-world objects, locations, people, or organizations that can be denoted with a proper noun.

cards.title: Spotting the Clues
cards.body:
Have you ever wondered how search engines instantly know that "Paris" in your search is a destination, while "Paris Hilton" is a person? 

It's not magic—it is all thanks to a smart language system called Named Entity Recognition (NER)! Let's learn how AI detects these details on the fly.

cards.title: What is NER?
cards.body:
Named Entity Recognition (NER) is an AI pipeline that identifies and classifies key information (entities) in text. 

As the model reads a sentence, it processes each word (token) through its network. At the very end, a math step called a **softmax layer** calculates probabilities to assign the most likely label (like Person, Organization, or Location) to each word.

cards.title: The Post Office Sorter
cards.body:
Imagine a postal worker sorting a pile of letters. They read through the mail and quickly stick labels on them: "House" for home addresses, "Business" for company names, and "Person" for the recipient. 

An NER pipeline does exactly this! It reads a sentence word-by-word and immediately slaps a category sticker (like Location or Organization) on each word.

cards.title: NER in Action
cards.body:
Let's look at how an NER model tags this sentence:
"Ada Lovelace visited London in 1843."

The model processes the tokens and labels them:
• **Ada Lovelace** ➔ PERSON
• **visited** ➔ Outside (Not an entity)
• **London** ➔ LOCATION
• **in 1843** ➔ TIME / Outside

cards.title: Is it Just a Dictionary?
cards.body:
Many people think NER models just look up words in a giant pre-made dictionary. But that's not true! 

If you write "The Apple fell from the tree," a smart NER model knows "Apple" is a fruit (not an organization) because it looks at the surrounding words, rather than just matching "Apple" to a static list of companies.

cards.title: Token-by-Token vs. Whole Sentence
cards.body: See how the model assigns tags to each individual token using its prediction layers, instead of looking at the whole sentence as a single unit.

right.text: [Whole Sentence] -> [Single Classifier] -> [One Label: Sports, Finance, Sci-Fi]

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of a Named Entity Recognition (NER) system?

options.text: To determine if a review is positive or negative.
To translate sentences from English to Spanish.
To locate and classify names, places, and organizations in a text.
To correct spelling and grammar errors in a paragraph.

exercises.explain: NER is designed to extract specific entities (names, places, organizations) rather than summarizing the overall theme or sentiment of the text.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An NER model outputs a classification label for each individual token in the input sentence.
exercises.explain: The model evaluates tokens step-by-step (or in parallel) and assigns a label prediction to each individual token using its final classification layer.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing component of the NER pipeline that outputs label probabilities.
exercises.sentence: To decide which entity label a token gets, the model passes the token's features through a [blank] layer.
exercises.explain: The softmax layer converts the raw output numbers of the network into probabilities for each label, allowing the model to choose the best category for each word.

exercises.prompt: Which AI model correctly identified the entities in this sentence: "Google is headquartered in New York."?
exercises.explain: AI output A correctly classifies "Google" as an Organization and "New York" as a Location. AI output B mistakenly labels "Google" as a Person and "New York" as an Organization.

exercises.prompt: How does the softmax layer at the end of an NER pipeline determine the entity label for a specific word token?

options.text: It combines all words into a single sentence category summary.
It outputs a probability distribution over the possible entity tags for each token.
It translates the words into another language before tagging.
It randomly assigns labels to speed up the process.

exercises.explain: In an NER model, the final layer evaluates each word token individually. The Softmax function takes the scores for all possible entity types (like Person, Org, Loc, or None) and converts them into probabilities, choosing the highest one for that specific token.
